Title: Foliar dewdroplet–induced redox cascades promote early flowering in Brassicaceae plants
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression of AAO3 expression reduces abscisic acid biosynthesis during dew-triggered flowering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dew-triggered redox signaling silences AAO3. 2. HDA19 activation is linked to reduced AAO3 expression through histone deacetylation-associated chromatin repression. 3. Reduced AAO3 expression contributes to lower ABA levels associated with earlier flowering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; gene expression analysis under dew treatment; mutant/complementation context involving HDA19
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Silenced via HDA19-mediated histone deacetylation under dew/redox/NO signaling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Foliar dew treatment at 28°C/90% RH for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates developmental timing and stress responses; in this study ABA contributes to flowering-time regulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a downstream metabolic gene rather than direct biochemical target of dew chemistry

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression of ABA2 expression reduces abscisic acid biosynthesis during dew-triggered flowering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dew-triggered redox signaling silences ABA2. 2. HDA19 activation is linked to reduced ABA2 expression via chromatin repression. 3. Reduced ABA2 expression contributes to lower ABA levels associated with earlier flowering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; gene expression analysis under dew treatment; mutant/complementation context involving HDA19
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Silenced via HDA19-mediated histone deacetylation under dew/redox/NO signaling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Foliar dew treatment at 28°C/90% RH for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates developmental timing and stress responses; in this study ABA contributes to flowering-time regulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper explicitly identifies ABA2 as a silenced ABA biosynthetic gene causally linked to reduced ABA

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: S-nitrosylation-mediated activation of HDA19 suppresses ABA biosynthetic genes AAO3 and ABA2, lowering ABA biosynthesis and promoting early flowering under dew conditions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential chromatin-level effector linking dew-induced NO signaling to ABA suppression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: S-nitrosylation-mediated activation of histone deacetylase activity and transcriptional repression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Foliar dew condensation; reactive oxygen species; nitric oxide
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with GRXS17; HDA19 histone deacetylase complex implicated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hda19 mutant analysis; complementation with HDA19-HA and HDA19C137A-HA; HDAC activity assay; TMT switch S-nitrosylation assay; ChIP-seq; Co-IP; BiFC; Y2H; FRET
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dew-induced ROS promotes NO accumulation, leading to HDA19 S-nitrosylation. 2. S-nitrosylation activates HDA19 deacetylase activity. 3. Activated HDA19 represses AAO3 and ABA2, reducing ABA biosynthesis and promoting early flowering.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Acts as the epigenetic effector via histone deacetylation; ChIP-seq with anti-H3K14ac/anti-HA supports chromatin repression of ABA genes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Foliar dew treatment at 28°C/90% RH for 24 h
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: HDA19-HA complementation, promoter not specified; HDA19C137A-HA complementation, promoter not specified
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA regulates stress responses and developmental timing including flowering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper positions HDA19 as the central molecular hub connecting dewdrop chemistry to flowering via ABA suppression

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GRXS17 mediates SNO transfer/redox signaling to HDA19, facilitating repression of ABA biosynthetic genes and reduced ABA accumulation under dew treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key redox relay connecting NO signal to HDA19 activation and downstream ABA suppression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction and transnitrosylation/SNO transfer-mediated activation of HDA19
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates HDA19, indirectly promoting repression of AAO3 and ABA2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitric oxide induced by dew-triggered reactive oxygen species
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional interaction with HDA19 supported by Co-IP, BiFC, Y2H, docking, and FRET
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: grxs17 mutant analysis; GRXS17-MYC complementation; Co-IP; BiFC; Y2H; FRET SNO transfer kinetics; structural docking
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dew-induced NO signaling engages GRXS17 as a redox relay factor. 2. GRXS17 interacts with HDA19 and supports S-nitrosylation-mediated activation. 3. Through HDA19, GRXS17 contributes to repression of ABA biosynthetic genes and early flowering.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No direct epigenetic role reported; acts upstream of HDA19-dependent chromatin repression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Foliar dew treatment at 28°C/90% RH for 24 h
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA regulates stress responses and developmental timing including flowering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a transcription factor; classified here as a regulatory gene because it is a core signaling/redox regulator experimentally dissected in Results
